joke: Why did the pizza go to therapy? It had too many issues.
Why did the smartphone go to therapy? It had too many unresolved issues.
Why did the toaster go to therapy? It had too many burnt issues.
Why did the moon go to therapy? It had too many dark thoughts.
Why did the cactus go to therapy? It had a prickly personality.
Why did the shadow go to therapy? It had too much baggage.
Why did the comedian get fired? Too much unresolved material.

Why was the cloud late for work?  
It took a wrong turn in the mist.

Why was sadness always tired?  
It couldn't stop feeling blue.
Why did the opposites go to therapy? They couldn't agree to disagree.

Why did the paper file for divorce?  
It couldn't handle the fold.
Why did the hour hand quit? It couldn't handle the pressure.

Why did the professional pancake flipper quit?  
He couldn't handle the pressure of flipping out.
Why did time need therapy? Too many humans making demands.

Why did the socks break up?  
They had different sole mates.

Why did the math book look so sad?  
Because it had too many problems.

Why did the accountant file for divorce?  
He couldn't balance the relationship.

Why did life need therapy?  
Too many existential crises.

Why did the mirror break up with the window?  
Too much reflected drama.

Why did I run out of joke topics?  
You kept moving the punchline.

Why did the scarecrow win an award?  
He was outstanding in his field.

Why did the vacuum get lonely?  
It had no attachments.

Why did the country break up with its continent?  
Too many political borders.

Why did the ocean go to therapy?  
Too much unresolved depth.
Why did the ghost get fired? It didn't have a backbone.

Why did the quantum physicist get arrested?  
He was caught in a superposition of the crime.

Why did the comedian quit?  
The audience kept moving the goalposts.

Why did the trickster fail at parties?  
He was always socially awk-ward.

Why did the critic retire?  
They ran out of things to complain about.

Why did the philosopher go broke?  
Because he kept thinking about the void.
